items.title: Iran will oversee reopening and operation of Strait of Hormuz, foreign minister says
items.author: Associated Press
items.content:
<article>Iranian Foreign Minister Abbas Araghchi on Sunday insisted his country would oversee the reopening and continued operation of the Strait of Hormuz. (AP video shot by Ali Jabar)

items.author: Associated Press
items.content:
<article>Croatia and Ghana fans celebrated together outside Philadelphia Stadium as they danced and waved their national flags. (AP video by Tassanee Vejpongsa)

items.title: Albanians keep protesting against Kushner-linked resort project
items.author: Associated Press
items.content:
<article>Thousands of Albanians continued to protest against a coastal development project linked to Donald Trump’s son-in-law Jared Kushner, on Saturday. The proposed luxury resort could threaten flamingo habitats as well as other migratory birds. (AP Video by Eri Xhabafti)

#albania #news

items.title: Serbia's President Vucic says he will resign within weeks as election nears
items.author: Associated Press
items.content:
<article>Serbia's populist President Aleksandar Vucic said Saturday he will resign the post within weeks, apparently to pave the way for early elections following youth-led protests. Vucic did not specify when he would resign or when the vote could be held.

#serbia #news

Read more: https://apnews.com​

items.title: Funeral held for 1-year-old killed by police in Mississippi
items.author: Associated Press
items.content:
<article>A family in Mississippi held a funeral Saturday for their 1-year-old child who was killed when police fired into a moving vehicle. The shooting has sparked outrage in the small city of Senatobia, where some say it’s the latest in a series of troubling encounters between police and Black residents.

#mississippi #news

Read more: https://apnews.com​

This video may be available for archive licensing via https://newsroom.ap.org/home</article>

items.title: Largest wildfire in the US spreads through tinder-dry forest in Utah
items.author: Associated Press
items.content:
<article>The largest fire in the United States has spread through tens of thousands of square miles of tinder-dry forest in Utah, fueled by conditions that fire managers and experts call unprecedented. (AP video by Ty O'Neil)

items.content:
<article>Sirens sounded in Kuwait City early on Sunday after both Bahrain and Kuwait said Iran targeted their nations with drone and missile fire.

items.title: Frustration grows in Venezuela as earthquake death toll reaches 1,430
items.author: Associated Press
items.content:
<article>Tensions flared Saturday as desperation grew in Venezuela's state of La Guaira as rescuers and civilians searched for earthquake survivors and the death toll rose sharply to 1,430. (AP Video: Juan Arraez, Brayan Antequero, Randolfo Blanco, Matías Delacroix)

items.title: US Central Command releases video of strikes on Iran military targets
items.author: Associated Press
items.content:
<article>U.S. Central Command released video early Sunday purporting to show strikes on Iranian military targets. Footage showed multiple strikes on what the command said was "10 Iranian military targets at multiple locations in and near the Strait of Hormuz."

items.title: Could Wimbledon's new AI app features replace sports commentators?
items.author: Associated Press
items.content:
<article>From AI generated match analysis to a chatbot that can answer questions about players and live matches, Wimbledon has been expanding the role of AI across its app and website. The new features are designed to help fans understand not just what's happening on court, but why a match might be developing the way it is. Could Wimbledon's new AI app features replace sports commentators?

Subscribe: http://smarturl.it/AssociatedPress 
Read more: https://apnews.com​

This video may be available for archive licensing via https://newsroom.ap.org/home</article>

items.title: AP video captures Israel strikes hitting central Gaza
items.author: Associated Press
items.content:
<article>AP footage captured the moment that Israeli strikes hit Deir al Balah, in Central Gaza Strip on Saturday evening. No casualties were immediately reported.

items.title: Venezuelans search rubble as anger grows over earthquake response
items.author: Associated Press
items.content:
<article>Desperation set in on Saturday among Venezuelans trying to locate their loved ones alive among piles of rubble, where public housing buildings and ocean view towers stood until twin powerful earthquakes brought them down midweek. People in the community of Caraballeda went so far as to block an excavator from leaving the site of a collapse and to pull the operator from its cabin shortly after state workers took selfies in front of flattened buildings and left without having helped at all. (AP Video shot by Andry Rincón)

#venezuela #earthquake #news 

Subscribe: http://smarturl.it/AssociatedPress 
Read more: https://apnews.com​

This video may be available for archive licensing via https://newsroom.ap.org/home</article>
